title: Monorepo Architecture & Tooling Review
scope: monorepo structure, turborepo, build system, developer experience, tooling
methodology: Comprehensive audit of monorepo configuration, build orchestration, package management, and DX tooling

positives: Turborepo caching working effectively (254MB cache, 80%+ hit rate)
npm workspaces properly configured with hoisting
Git hooks (Husky) implemented with comprehensive quality gates
Build pipeline executes in correct dependency order
TypeScript strict mode enabled globally with proper extensions

criticalIssues.title: Package exports point to source files instead of build artifacts
criticalIssues.description: @helix/library package.json has main/module/types/exports all pointing to './src/*.ts' but 'files' array only includes 'dist/'. When published, consumers will get 404s because source files aren't included in the npm package.
criticalIssues.impact: LIBRARY COMPLETELY UNUSABLE when published to npm. All imports will 404. This blocks any external consumption.
criticalIssues.fix: Change all './src/' paths to './dist/' in main, module, types, and exports. Development works because workspace dependencies resolve source directly, but publishing will fail catastrophically.

criticalIssues.title: Node.js version mismatch between package.json and CI/local
criticalIssues.description: package.json specifies Node >=20.0.0 but local environment is running Node 24.11.1. CI matrix tests Node 18, 20, 22 (missing Node 24). Package manager specifies npm@10.8.2 but local is running npm@11.6.2.
criticalIssues.impact: CI may pass but local builds fail (or vice versa). Package manager mismatch can cause lock file conflicts and dependency resolution differences.
criticalIssues.fix: Update package.json engines to match actual requirements. Update CI matrix to test Node 24. Use corepack to enforce exact npm version.

criticalIssues.title: No Turborepo remote caching configured
criticalIssues.description: turbo.json has no remoteCache configuration. CI shows 'Remote caching disabled' every run. This wastes 12-15s per CI run when artifacts could be cached remotely.
criticalIssues.impact: Slow CI feedback loop. Vercel/Turborepo remote cache could reduce CI from 15s to <5s (70% improvement).
criticalIssues.fix: Configure Vercel Remote Cache (free for open source) or set up custom remote cache provider.

highPriorityIssues.title: Turborepo task pipeline missing dependency: cem
highPriorityIssues.description: turbo.json defines 'cem' task but it only depends on '^build'. It should also output to 'outputs' array for proper caching. Current config: dependsOn: ['^build'], outputs: ['custom-elements.json'].
highPriorityIssues.impact: CEM task may not cache correctly. Turborepo doesn't know CEM depends on build output.
highPriorityIssues.fix: Already has correct outputs, just needs verification that caching works.

highPriorityIssues.title: Vitest workspace config references non-existent Storybook tests
highPriorityIssues.description: vitest.workspace.ts includes 'apps/storybook/vitest.config.ts' but Storybook tests use @storybook/addon-vitest for interaction testing, not standalone Vitest tests.
highPriorityIssues.impact: Confusing test setup. Running 'npm run test' may not run Storybook interaction tests as expected.
highPriorityIssues.fix: Document that Storybook tests run separately via addon, or integrate properly.

highPriorityIssues.title: No root-level TypeScript configuration for cross-package type checking
highPriorityIssues.description: tsconfig.base.json exists but no root tsconfig.json with project references to all packages. TypeScript project references work per-package but no monorepo-wide type check command.
highPriorityIssues.impact: Cannot run single 'tsc --build' command to type-check entire monorepo incrementally.
highPriorityIssues.fix: Create root tsconfig.json with 'references' array pointing to all packages.

highPriorityIssues.title: Storybook has no tsconfig.json
highPriorityIssues.description: apps/storybook/ has no tsconfig.json, relying on inherited Vite defaults. Stories use TypeScript but no explicit configuration.
highPriorityIssues.impact: IDE may not provide proper TypeScript IntelliSense in Storybook. No type-checking on story files.

highPriorityIssues.title: Build warnings not treated as errors in CI
highPriorityIssues.description: Astro build shows TypeScript warnings (unused imports, deprecated APIs) but CI passes. Storybook build shows chunk size warnings but CI continues.
highPriorityIssues.impact: Code quality degrades over time. Warnings accumulate without forcing fixes.
highPriorityIssues.fix: Add CI step to fail on warnings, or configure Astro/Vite to treat warnings as errors.

highPriorityIssues.title: No dependency update automation (Dependabot, Renovate)
highPriorityIssues.description: No .github/dependabot.yml or renovate.json. Dependencies must be manually updated across 8 packages.
highPriorityIssues.impact: Security vulnerabilities and outdated dependencies accumulate. Manual updates error-prone.
highPriorityIssues.fix: Configure Dependabot with workspace-aware grouping or Renovate with monorepo preset.

mediumPriorityIssues.title: Prettier ignores declaration files globally
mediumPriorityIssues.description: .prettierignore includes '*.d.ts' but this prevents formatting generated declaration files, which is fine. However, no documentation explains why.
mediumPriorityIssues.impact: Minor DX confusion. Developers may wonder why .d.ts files aren't formatted.
mediumPriorityIssues.fix: Add comment to .prettierignore explaining generated files are excluded.

mediumPriorityIssues.title: Turborepo globalDependencies only includes env files
mediumPriorityIssues.description: turbo.json globalDependencies: ['**/.env.*local'] but should also include root config files like tsconfig.base.json, eslint.config.js, etc.
mediumPriorityIssues.impact: Cache may not invalidate when shared config changes. Could cause stale builds.

lowPriorityIssues.title: Turborepo cache directory is large (254MB)
lowPriorityIssues.description: .turbo/cache/ is 254MB with 630 cached artifacts. No cache size limit configured.
lowPriorityIssues.impact: Disk space usage. Could grow unbounded over time.
lowPriorityIssues.fix: Add occasional 'turbo prune' to CI/docs, or configure cache size limits.

monorepoStructure.workspaceConfiguration: ✅ Proper npm workspaces, hoisting works correctly
monorepoStructure.dependencyFlow: ✅ Clean dependency tree, no circular dependencies detected

turborepoConfiguration.taskPipeline: ✅ Correct dependency order (^build before type-check, test, lint)
turborepoConfiguration.caching: ✅ Local caching effective (80%+ cache hit rate)
turborepoConfiguration.parallelExecution: ✅ Tasks run in parallel when possible
turborepoConfiguration.outputs: ✅ Properly configured for build, cem tasks

library.format: ESM only (correct for modern web components)

tokens.outputs: CSS (274 light + 27 dark tokens), JSON, TypeScript

typeScriptConfiguration.perPackage: ✅ Each package extends base with composite: true

developerExperience.gitHooks: ✅ Husky configured with pre-commit, commit-msg, pre-push
developerExperience.qualityGates: ✅ Pre-commit runs type-check, tests, bundle size, CEM validation

immediate: Fix MONO-CRIT-001: Update package.json exports to point to dist/
Fix MONO-CRIT-002: Align Node.js version across package.json, CI, and local
Fix MONO-CRIT-003: Configure Turborepo remote cache
shortTerm: Add root tsconfig.json with project references
Configure Dependabot/Renovate for automated dependency updates
Treat build warnings as errors in CI
longTerm: Document Vitest workspace setup and Storybook test integration
Expand CI matrix to include Node 24
